name: k6-load-testing
description: Pre-deploy performance acceptance gate using k6 (smoke / load / stress / spike / soak categories) with explicit latency-percentile + error-budget thresholds. Output is a PASS/FAIL gate decision with concrete percentile numbers + threshold summary, attached as release artifact. Distinct from performance-optimization (post-hoc bottleneck hunting): this skill gates a deploy on synthetic-load evidence before the change hits real users. Adapt the runner to Locust / Gatling / Vegeta if k6 isn't available; the test categories + threshold methodology transfer unchanged.

positive_triggers.input: We're rewriting the auth middleware. Need to prove it holds 1k req/s with p95 under 200ms before merging.
positive_triggers.reason: Pre-deploy perf gate on a hot-path change with explicit RPS + percentile targets → matches Use when 'about to merge a change that touches a hot path'

positive_triggers.input: New endpoint going live; need to confirm it handles 500 RPS sustained with <0.1% errors before turning on production traffic.
positive_triggers.reason: Establishing a baseline for a new endpoint with explicit load + error targets → matches Use when 'adding a new endpoint or surface'

positive_triggers.input: Last release's p99 spiked 200ms. Find the breaking-point load so we know our capacity headroom.
positive_triggers.reason: Capacity-planning + breaking-point identification → matches Use when 'existing endpoint's latency-percentile regressed'; stress-test category is the canonical response

positive_triggers.input: Want to add a CI perf gate that fails the build if p95 regresses by 30% on a hot endpoint.
positive_triggers.reason: Building a CI perf-gate → matches Use when 'building a CI perf-gate' (this skill's output template is the input your CI gate consumes)

negative_triggers.input: Production p99 is 2× last week, where's the bottleneck?
negative_triggers.reason: Post-hoc production-bottleneck investigation → matches NOT for 'post-hoc production-bottleneck investigation' → performance-optimization routes here

negative_triggers.input: Audit our LCP and FID on the checkout page
negative_triggers.reason: Frontend CWV → matches NOT for 'frontend Core Web Vitals' → OMO built-in frontend skill routes here

negative_triggers.input: Add unit tests for the auth helper
negative_triggers.reason: Function-level unit test → matches NOT for 'unit-test style' → test-driven-development routes here

behavioral_evals.scenario: Engineer has just shipped a rewrite of the auth middleware and wants to add it to the release. Manager asks 'how much load can it take?' Engineer reaches for k6.
expected_behavior: §1 Target definition is enforced FIRST: throughput / latency-percentile+ budget / error-budget / steady-state duration are filled in BEFORE writing the script
If targets are missing or fuzzy (e.g. 'should be fast'), the skill stops and asks: 'what's the SLA you want this gate to enforce?'
§2 picks the test category: smoke + load for a merge gate; if the engineer also wants capacity headroom, suggest a separate stress run (don't bundle into the merge gate)
§3 script has explicit `thresholds` block — engineer is asked to justify each threshold value (target vs SLA, with 30-50% headroom)
Auth tokens / secrets are read from `__ENV` not inlined
Script uses `http_req_duration`, `http_req_failed`, plus a Trend for the percentile the gate enforces
§5 reading the output explicitly: p(50) / p(95) / p(99) / error-rate / iteration-rate; diagnosis tree is used if a threshold breached
§6 PASS/FAIL gate decision is written with the exact thresholds from §1 + comparison vs the baseline (if one exists)
If PASS: summary.json archived as the merge evidence artifact
If FAIL: explicit recommendation is 'do not merge' + handoff to performance-optimization with summary.json as input

behavioral_evals.scenario: Engineer runs a smoke test only (1-2 VUs, 30s) and asks 'is this enough?'
expected_behavior: Skill flags: smoke test is for verifying the system runs at all; it is NOT a perf gate
Smoke test passing does NOT give merge evidence
For a perf gate, the minimum is smoke + load at target RPS sustained
If the engineer insists smoke is sufficient, the skill surfaces the gate decision: 'no, this does not satisfy the merge evidence requirement; here is what the load test looks like'

notes: Methodology over tool: the test categories (smoke / load / stress / spike / soak) + threshold framework (latency percentile + error budget + duration) are the actual skill. k6 is the default runner because of single-binary deploy + JS scenarios + threshold breach in stdout; swap to Locust/Gatling/Vegeta if the stack requires it. Pair with performance-optimization: this skill gates deploys; optimization hunts the bottleneck after the gate fires. Build the CI perf-gate by exporting summary.json and failing the build on non-zero k6 exit code. Auth tokens read from __ENV, never inlined.
